title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
summary: Ornith is a new open family of agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, with variants ranging from 9B to 397B parameters. Built on Gemma and Qwen, the models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with MIT licensing and GGUF versions for local deployment.
writeup: The video covers Ornith, a new open family of agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, with variants spanning 9B to 397B parameters and built on Gemma and Qwen. The models target coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, ship under MIT licensing, and offer GGUF versions for local deployment. The framing pairs these models with Hermes and Zed to assemble a fully private, locally run coding agent. The takeaway: open weights plus local GGUF support and permissive licensing make on-machine agentic coding possible without sending data to external services.
